summary: Russia launched nearly 200 drones against Ukraine overnight, targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions with civilian casualties reported. Ukrainian forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, ships in Kronstadt, the Progress plant in Tambov Oblast, and halted oil pumping at Zenzevatka station in Volgograd Oblast. NATO Secretary General Rutte arrived in Kyiv while European nations explore reopening diplomatic channels with Russia.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine with nearly 200 drones overnight, killing one woman in Kherson and injuring at least ten others (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Ukrainian forces struck a key oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, ships in Kronstadt, and the Progress plant in Tambov Oblast (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)
Russian forces used white phosphorus munitions on Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast, setting fire to residential areas (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)
Russian MoD reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast producing components for long-range attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
NATO Secretary General Rutte arrived in Kyiv for talks with Ukrainian leadership (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30638)
European nations, encouraged by France, are seeking to reopen a political dialogue channel with Russia (Source: @tass_agency/378845)
Ukrainian media report Russia has stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon missiles capable of reaching Kyiv in six minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614)
Russian forces are intensifying efforts near Krasnaya Liman, Sviatogorsk, and along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka on the Slavyansk front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine conducted a significant long-range strike campaign against Russian strategic infrastructure, hitting the Saint Petersburg oil terminal, Kronstadt naval port, the Progress defense plant in Tambov, and halting oil transfers at Zenzevatka in Volgograd Oblast. Russia retaliated with a massive overnight drone assault of nearly 200 UAVs targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson, killing one civilian and injuring at least ten. On the ground, Russian forces intensified urban combat operations around Krasnaya Liman, Sviatogorsk, and Kostyantynivka, the latter struck with white phosphorus munitions.

points: Ukrainian Defense Forces struck a major oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, one of Russia's key oil product transshipment hubs in the Baltic region, causing a fire on the same day as the city's economic forum (Source: @UkraineNow/81640)

Ukrainian forces also reportedly struck ships and port infrastructure in Kronstadt, hit the Progress plant in Tambov Oblast that produces components for high-precision weapons, and halted oil pumping at the Zenzevatka station in Volgograd Oblast (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)

Ukrainian media report Russia has stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon missiles for strikes on Kyiv, which can reach the capital in six minutes and are claimed to be impossible to intercept (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614)
MP Fedienko proposed creating a public register of draft dodgers to exert psychological pressure on evaders (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30617)

Ombudsman Lubinets warned that the upcoming TCC reform will not reduce violations, citing continued use of force, arrests, confiscation of belongings, and operations by masked personnel (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30624)

Ombudsman Lubinets criticized the lack of state funding for public shelters, noting that condominium associations bear the financial burden of maintaining shelters without any government compensation mechanism (Source: @UkraineNow/81629)

Head of the Presidential Office Budanov is reportedly responsible for the negotiation track with Russia, leveraging contacts built during his time leading military intelligence (Source: @UkraineNow/81644)
Russia launched nearly 200 drones against Ukraine overnight, targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson regions with civilian infrastructure strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Russian MoD reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced components for long-range attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
Russian forces used white phosphorus munitions on residential quarters of Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast, causing widespread fires (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)

Russian forces are intensifying efforts near Krasnaya Liman with urban battles, fighting on Sviatogorsk's outskirts, and advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka, 12 km from Slavyansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
A passenger bus traveling from Podolsk to Simferopol was attacked by Ukrainian forces near the center of Yenakiieve in DNR (Source: @tass_agency/378834)
European nations, pushed by France, are seeking to reopen a political communication channel with Russia separate from the existing NATO-Russia channel (Source: @tass_agency/378845)
In the Krasnaya Liman area, urban battles are underway with massive UAV use by both sides, and fighting continues on the northwestern outskirts of Sviatogorsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)

On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces are advancing along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka, with battles reported in Ray-Aleksandrovka, an important Ukrainian defensive hub (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Intense urban battles continue in Konstantinovka, where Russian forces are actively pressing to expand control of surrounding areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Russian forces used white phosphorus munitions on Kostyantynivka's residential quarters, with dozens of incendiary elements falling on the city (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)

In Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, the Russian Vostok group is advancing in forested areas beyond the Volchya River northwest of Oleksandrihrad, consolidating a bridgehead north of the river (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
Enemy tactical aviation was active on the northern and southeastern directions overnight (Source: @kpszsu/63767)
Multiple drone groups detected heading toward Zhytomyr Oblast from the north, with one reactive drone tracked from Ovruch toward Korosten (Source: @eRadarrua/95195)
Kyiv Oblast saw four drones moving past the reservoir toward the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone, and three reactive shaheds were reported heading toward Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/95195)
Chernihiv Oblast had drones entering from Kulykivka, Ichnya, and Ripky, with additional drones tracked toward Slavutych and the ChZV area (Source: @eRadarrua/95186)
Sumy Oblast saw new drones heading toward Hlukhiv and Bilopillia (Source: @eRadarrua/95195)
Drones were detected on the border of Chernihiv Oblast and Belarus heading south (Source: @kpszsu/63765)
Odesa region was struck by drones overnight, with a truck and a service room damaged at a parking lot; two men injured, one hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
In Zaporizhzhia Oblast, a strike hit a gas station territory in Zaporizhzhia district, causing a fire; no casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)

Dnipropetrovsk Oblast was attacked over 15 times by drones and artillery, damaging a residential building, infrastructure, an agricultural enterprise, and a store across Nikopolsky, Kamiansky, and Samarivsky districts (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Kherson was shelled by artillery and drones across Dnipro, Korabelny, and Central districts, killing one woman and injuring at least ten including a 17-year-old boy (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Drones were tracked from Kherson Oblast heading toward or past Bereznehuvate in Mykolaiv Oblast (Source: @kpszsu/63733)
A drone heading toward Chornomorske from the Black Sea was detected (Source: @kpszsu/63757)
Russia launched nearly 200 drones against Ukraine in a single night across multiple regions including Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Enemy tactical aviation was active on the northern and southeastern directions (Source: @kpszsu/63767)
Multiple reactive shahed drones were tracked across Zhytomyr, Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Sumy oblasts, with three reactive shaheds heading toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67464)
A drone-relay type UAV was identified circling near Sofiyivka at approximately 150 km/h, not a reactive type (Source: @eRadarrua/95171)
Ukrainian media report Russia has stockpiled over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles for strikes on Kyiv, reaching the capital in six minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30614)
Drones were tracked on Dnipropetrovsk Oblast heading toward Solone (Source: @kpszsu/63737)

key_developments: Ukrainian forces struck the Saint Petersburg oil terminal, a key Russian oil transshipment hub in the Baltic region, along with ships in Kronstadt port, the Progress defense plant in Tambov, and halted oil pumping at Zenzevatka station in Volgograd Oblast (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)

Russia launched nearly 200 drones against Ukraine overnight targeting Odesa, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kherson, killing one woman in Kherson and injuring at least ten people including a 17-year-old boy (Source: @UkraineNow/81639)
Russian forces used white phosphorus munitions on residential quarters of Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast, causing widespread fires from incendiary elements (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)

Russian forces intensified urban combat near Krasnaya Liman and Sviatogorsk, advanced along the M-03 highway toward Yurkovka on the Slavyansk front, and the Vostok group consolidated a bridgehead beyond the Volchya River in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30635)
NATO Secretary General Rutte arrived in Kyiv for high-level consultations with Ukrainian leadership (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30638)
European nations, encouraged by France, are exploring reopening a political communication channel with Russia separate from the existing NATO-Russia diplomatic track (Source: @tass_agency/378845)
Russian MoD reported striking a Fire Point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced components for Ukrainian long-range attack UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is entering a phase of mutual strategic infrastructure targeting, with Ukraine demonstrating expanded long-range strike capability against Russian energy and defense industrial assets deep in Russian territory, including Saint Petersburg, Kronstadt, Tambov, and Volgograd. Russia continues to leverage mass drone swarms against Ukrainian civilian infrastructure, with nearly 200 UAVs deployed in a single night, while employing banned white phosphorus munitions on Kostyantynivka. The reported stockpiling of over 200 Tsirkon hypersonic missiles poses a severe threat to Kyiv, given their six-minute flight time and claimed inability to be intercepted. Ground operations remain intense in the Donetsk axis, with Russian forces pressing on multiple axes around Slavyansk, Krasnaya Liman, and Kostyantynivka.
sections.regional_response: NATO Secretary General Rutte's visit to Kyiv signals continued high-level alliance engagement, while former NATO Secretary General Stoltenberg warned that Europe remains mentally unprepared for a major war despite witnessing one on its doorstep. European nations, led by France, are actively exploring reopening a diplomatic channel with Russia, reflecting growing European fatigue and a desire for political engagement separate from NATO structures. The US is reportedly drawing down its military contingent in Lithuania, which had been increased to 1,000 soldiers with armored vehicles after the start of the conflict, with replacement timing and composition still unknown. Slovakia's President Pellegrini stated that peace negotiations should be a prerequisite for discussing Ukraine's EU membership.
sections.intelligence_notes: The creation of the ARES (Allied Reform and Expert Support) council, comprising retired NATO military leaders, indicates deepening Western institutional involvement in Ukrainian military reform and operational planning, with the dual purpose of improving Ukrainian capabilities and extracting lessons for NATO's own reform. A drone-relay type UAV was identified operating near Sofiyivka at approximately 150 km/h, suggesting Russia is deploying communication relay drones to extend UAV operational range and coordination. The attack on a bus traveling from Podolsk to Simferopol near Yenakiieve highlights the continued risk to civilian transport in and near the conflict zone. Ukraine's Ombudsman raised serious concerns about the effectiveness of TCC reforms, noting that masked personnel continue to conduct forced mobilizations with confiscation of personal belongings despite official orders to the contrary.
